title: The 'Start Monday' Trap Is Costing You $187,000
hook: You've promised yourself you'd start saving 'next week' hundreds of times — and that one habit has a six-figure price tag.
angle: Hyperbolic discounting is a known bias but almost never shown as a specific compounding dollar loss, broken down to a per-year delay cost anyone can feel.
audience: 25-40 year olds who know they should be investing but keep postponing
key_numbers: $187,000 — cost of starting at 28 instead of 25 ($400/mo, 7% return to 65)
$58,000 — future wealth burned per year of delay
87% — share of non-savers who say they plan to start soon
3.2 years — average actual delay before first retirement contribution
7 in 10 — non-savers caught in the intention-action gap
